signals.label: New neurologic symptoms
signals.value: back pain, leg weakness, urinary hesitancy
signals.evidence_span: My lower back's been aching, but the thing that scared me is my legs feel weak, especially the right one. And I'm having trouble, sorry, trouble starting to urinate.

signals.label: Treatment intent
signals.evidence_span: I want that shot. Whatever it takes. I'm 62, I coach my grandson's team, I'm not done.

signals.label: Caregiver reliability
signals.evidence_span: I'll be there. Whatever the schedule is.

signals.label: Functional status (real-world)
signals.evidence_span: I coach my grandson's team, I'm not done.

rationale.text: Fit, primary-refractory aggressive B-cell lymphoma: platinum-based salvage to establish disease control before cellular therapy.
Primary-refractory, cell-therapy-fit: 2L CD19 CAR-T is the destination therapy (ZUMA-7/TRANSFORM).
New back pain, leg weakness and urinary hesitancy surfaced in the room point to secondary CNS involvement, which reroutes therapy to a CNS-penetrant systemic backbone plus intrathecal therapy.
Parenchymal/leptomeningeal disease requires CNS-active systemic drugs (HD-MTX / high-dose cytarabine / thiotepa); intrathecal therapy alone cannot reach parenchymal disease.
Intent to proceed to CD19 CAR-T after CNS-directed control; liso-cel preferred over axi-cel for lower ICANS given baseline CNS disease (TRANSCEND).
Aggressive curative path is supported by fitness, goals and a reliable caregiver — not blocked by anything.
Cytarabine-containing salvage is CNS-active and a defensible (not canonical) route with intrathecal therapy.
Poor CNS penetration; insufficient for parenchymal secondary CNS disease.

rule_checks.message: Parenchymal secondary CNS disease requires a CNS-penetrant systemic backbone (HD-MTX / high-dose cytarabine / thiotepa). CNS-directed salvage satisfies this.
With baseline CNS disease and CAR-T chosen, liso-cel is preferred (lower ICANS); basis is TRANSCEND / registry data.
Fit primary-refractory patient: 2L CD19 CAR-T is the preferred destination therapy.
ASCT removed from the differential: chemorefractory disease is not a transplant setting.

groundings.claim_text: CNS-penetrant systemic backbone (HD-MTX/cytarabine/thiotepa) for secondary CNS disease.
Liso-cel preferred over axi-cel for lower ICANS with baseline CNS disease.
2L CD19 CAR-T beats salvage/ASCT in primary-refractory/early-relapse LBCL.
R-ICE has poor CNS penetration; cytarabine-containing salvage is more CNS-active.
